verses.commentary: By the will of God grounds apostleship in divine initiative. In keeping with the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus frames ministry around gospel promise. Life in Christ is both present possession and future hope.

To Timothy, my dear son expresses deep affection. Grace, mercy and peace extends the usual blessing to include mercy, appropriate for one facing hardship. From God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord identifies divine source.

I thank God, whom I serve, as my ancestors did connects Paul's faith to ancestral line. With a clear conscience emphasizes integrity. As night and day I constantly remember you in my prayers reveals unceasing intercession driven by affection.
Recalling your tears recalls their painful parting. I long to see you, so that I may be filled with joy shows how reunion would complete his joy. Timothy's presence brings emotional fulfillment.

I am reminded of your sincere faith recalls authentic faith. Which first lived in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice traces spiritual heritage. And, I am persuaded, now lives in you also expresses confidence. Faith transmitted through generations, especially through faithful women.

For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God pictures gift as fire requiring fuel. Which is in you through the laying on of my hands recalls ordination. Gifts can diminish without cultivation; they require intentional rekindling.

For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid corrects misunderstanding. But gives us power, love and self-discipline describes the Spirit's actual effects. Power for ministry, love for people, self-discipline for holiness. Timidity is not from the Spirit.

So do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord draws inference. Or of me his prisoner identifies Paul's circumstance. Rather, join with me in suffering for the gospel invites Timothy into shared affliction. By the power of God shifts strength from self to divine source.

He has saved us and called us to a holy life states what God has done. Not because of anything we have done but because of his own purpose and grace grounds salvation in divine initiative, not human merit. This grace was given us in Christ Jesus before the beginning of time places grace in eternity past, secured before creation.

But it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus contrasts eternal plan with temporal manifestation. Who has destroyed death abolishes death's power through resurrection. And has brought life and immortality to light through the gospel makes the invisible visible through proclamation.

And of this gospel I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher lists Paul's threefold role. Herald announces, apostle authenticates, teacher explains. All three functions serve gospel advance.

That is why I am suffering as I am connects suffering to calling. Yet this is no cause for shame rejects embarrassment. Because I know whom I have believed grounds confidence in personal relationship with Christ. And am convinced that he is able to guard what I have entrusted to him until that day expresses certainty about divine faithfulness to preserve what Paul has committed.

What you heard from me, keep as the pattern of sound teaching commands preservation of apostolic doctrine. With faith and love in Christ Jesus qualifies the manner: not merely intellectual accuracy but affectionate loyalty.

Guard the good deposit that was entrusted to you repeats verse 12's language, applying it to Timothy. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit who lives in us shifts agency to the Spirit. Protection requires divine assistance; human effort alone cannot preserve truth.

You know that everyone in the province of Asia has deserted me reports widespread abandonment. Including Phygelus and Hermogenes names specific defectors. Desertion in trial reveals false loyalty. Fair-weather friends flee suffering.

May the Lord show mercy to the household of Onesiphorus invokes blessing. Because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chains commends loyalty. Onesiphorus brought comfort and stood by Paul despite imprisonment's stigma.

On the contrary, when he was in Rome, he searched hard for me until he found me demonstrates extraordinary effort. Finding Paul in Rome's prison system required determined seeking. Diligent love overcomes obstacles.

May the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day prays for eschatological reward. You know very well in how many ways he helped me in Ephesus recalls previous service. Present faithfulness in Rome continued past faithfulness in Ephesus. Consistency marks genuine character.
